title: Daughter of the Blood
author: Anne Bishop
description: The first Black Jewels novel: in a matriarchal dark-fantasy world where magical power is measured by the jewels witches wear, a prophesied queen arrives as a child.

title: Heir to the Shadows
author: Anne Bishop
description: Black Jewels #2: the child queen Jaenelle Angelline recovers from her trauma and begins to take her place as the most powerful witch in the Realms.

title: Queen of the Darkness
author: Anne Bishop
description: Black Jewels #3: the final confrontation with Dorothea SaDiablo — the war that will decide whether the dream of Witch becomes nightmare or fulfillment.

title: Written in Red
author: Anne Bishop
description: The first Others novel: a runaway blood prophet finds refuge in a community of shape-shifting terra indigene — the supernatural beings who tolerate humanity.

title: Murder of Crows
author: Anne Bishop
description: The Others #2: Meg Corbyn's growing visions force her and the shifters of the Lakeside Courtyard to confront a drug that turns humans homicidal around Others.

title: Spider's Bite
author: Jennifer Estep
description: The first Elemental Assassin novel: a stone and ice elemental assassin named Gin Blanco, who cooks barbecue by day, takes a contract that goes wrong.

title: Web of Lies
author: Jennifer Estep
description: Elemental Assassin #2: Gin gets drawn into a mining conspiracy in the Appalachian town she grew up in — and a reunion with a family she'd rather forget.

title: Venom
author: Jennifer Estep
description: Elemental Assassin #3: Gin's ex-mentor sends her a gift from beyond the grave and she must confront a fire elemental as strong as her sworn enemy.

title: A Discovery of Witches
author: Deborah Harkness
description: A witch-descended historian at Oxford accidentally unlocks a long-hidden alchemical manuscript and finds herself in an alliance — and romance — with a vampire.

title: Shadow of Night
author: Deborah Harkness
description: All Souls Trilogy #2: Diana Bishop and Matthew Clairmont travel to Elizabethan London to hunt for the missing Ashmole 782 and find Diana a magical teacher.

title: The Book of Life
author: Deborah Harkness
description: All Souls Trilogy #3: Diana and Matthew return to the present pursued by enemies on all sides, racing to reassemble Ashmole 782 before their unborn twins are born.

title: Tiger Eye
author: Marjorie M. Liu
description: The first Dirk & Steele paranormal romance: a Chinese-American antiques dealer buys an ancient riddle box at a Beijing flea market — and releases an immortal tiger-shifter warrior.

title: The Iron Hunt
author: Marjorie M. Liu
description: The first Hunter Kiss novel: a demon hunter's tattoos come alive at night as her personal bodyguards against the zombies of the apocalypse she is meant to prevent.

title: Monstress, Vol. 1: Awakening
author: Marjorie M. Liu
description: Liu's acclaimed graphic novel with Sana Takeda: a teenage girl in an alternate 1920s Asia shares a psychic link with a monstrous eldritch god — Eisner and Hugo winner.

title: Cast in Shadow
author: Michelle Sagara
description: The first Chronicles of Elantra novel: a young Hawk (police officer) in a multiracial fantasy city is sent to investigate child murders tied to her own traumatic past.

title: Cast in Courtlight
author: Michelle Sagara
description: Chronicles of Elantra #2: Kaylin Neya is summoned to the Barrani High Court as a diplomat and must survive immortal politics while keeping a dying prince alive.

title: Cast in Secret
author: Michelle Sagara
description: Chronicles of Elantra #3: Kaylin investigates an ancient artifact at the Elantra Exchange and uncovers a centuries-old elemental conspiracy.

title: The Red Tree
author: Caitlin R. Kiernan
description: A Rhode Island writer rents an isolated farmhouse to work through writer's block and discovers an unfinished manuscript about the enormous, ancient red oak in her backyard.

title: The Drowning Girl
author: Caitlin R. Kiernan
description: Kiernan's haunted novel of a schizophrenic woman's encounters with a strange woman who may be a mermaid, a werewolf, or a ghost — winner of the Bram Stoker Award.

title: Silk
author: Caitlin R. Kiernan
description: Kiernan's debut: a goth band in Birmingham, Alabama, is drawn into the growing psychosis of their keyboardist and the sinister things that begin crawling out of the walls.

title: Let the Right One In
author: John Ajvide Lindqvist
description: A bullied twelve-year-old boy in 1980s suburban Stockholm befriends a strange new neighbor who only comes out at night — Lindqvist's influential Swedish vampire novel.

title: Handling the Undead
author: John Ajvide Lindqvist
description: In Stockholm, the recently dead begin returning home — not as monsters but as empty, grieving bodies. Three families must decide what to do with their returned.

title: Harbor
author: John Ajvide Lindqvist
description: A father whose daughter vanished on a small Swedish island years ago returns to confront the sea itself — Lindqvist's quiet, relentless folk-horror novel.

title: Little Star
author: John Ajvide Lindqvist
description: Two traumatized teenage girls — one a famous TV discovery, one a dangerous forest-raised foundling — form a friendship that escalates into a nationwide horror.

title: Patient Zero
author: Jonathan Maberry
description: The first Joe Ledger novel: a Baltimore detective is recruited into a black-ops team hunting terrorists who have weaponized a fast-acting zombie virus.

title: The Dragon Factory
author: Jonathan Maberry
description: Joe Ledger #2: a genetic engineering cartel is breeding monsters — and a Nazi offshoot family is about to release a planet-killing pathogen.

title: Dead of Night
author: Jonathan Maberry
description: A Pennsylvania small-town cop and a reporter fight the first hours of a local zombie outbreak caused by a botched prison execution.

title: Ghost Road Blues
author: Jonathan Maberry
description: The first Pine Deep novel: a town that markets itself as America's Halloween capital is visited by three very real escaped killers — and something older beneath them.

title: Roadside Picnic
author: Arkady Strugatsky
description: Twelve years after alien 'visitors' stopped briefly on Earth and left inexplicable artifacts behind, the 'Stalkers' who venture into the Zones to retrieve them face unending physics.

title: Hard to Be a God
author: Arkady Strugatsky
description: A historian from future Earth is embedded as an observer on a feudal-era alien planet and must decide whether to intervene as a fascist takeover begins.

title: Monday Begins on Saturday
author: Arkady Strugatsky
description: A Soviet programmer takes a job at a research institute studying magic — a satirical science-fiction comedy aimed at Soviet academic bureaucracy.

title: The Doomed City
author: Arkady Strugatsky
description: The Strugatskys' banned-for-decades masterwork: a 1950s Leningrad Komsomol is recruited into a mysterious 'Experiment' in a city at the edge of an abyss.

title: Folding Beijing
author: Hao Jingfang
description: Hao's Hugo-winning novelette: a future Beijing physically folds itself into three shifts of population, and a worker from the bottom crosses the divide to carry a message.

title: Vagabonds
author: Hao Jingfang
description: A century after a Mars colony won independence in a brutal war, a cultural exchange program sends five teenage Martians to Earth — and they return no longer certain who they are.

title: Twelve Kings in Sharakhai
author: Bradley P. Beaulieu
description: The first Song of the Shattered Sands novel: in a desert city ruled by twelve immortal kings, a young pit fighter is drawn into a plot to assassinate them.

title: With Blood Upon the Sand
author: Bradley P. Beaulieu
description: Song of the Shattered Sands #2: Çeda enters the Maidens, the elite all-female warriors of the Kings, as a double agent determined to destroy them from within.

title: Beneath the Twisted Trees
author: Bradley P. Beaulieu
description: Song of the Shattered Sands #3: Çeda, now a fugitive in the desert, must forge alliances with the nomadic tribes as the Twelve Kings hunt her.

title: The Curse of the Mistwraith
author: Janny Wurts
description: The first Wars of Light and Shadow: two half-brothers inherit opposed gifts of light and shadow and must banish the Mistwraith suffocating their world — but their curse pits them against each other.

title: The Ships of Merior
author: Janny Wurts
description: Wars of Light and Shadow #2: the brothers' conflict metastasizes into full war as Arithon disappears to build a fleet and Lysaer raises armies to hunt him.

title: Warhost of Vastmark
author: Janny Wurts
description: Wars of Light and Shadow #3: Arithon defends the mountain clans of Vastmark against Lysaer's crusading army in the first massive battle of their war.

title: Midnight at the Well of Souls
author: Jack L. Chalker
description: The first Well World novel: a spaceship stumbles into an alien world designed to house 1,560 species in hexagonal habitats, and the visitors are transformed into aliens themselves.

title: Exiles at the Well of Souls
author: Jack L. Chalker
description: Well World #2: a pair of kidnapped Earth children sent to the Well World become central to a race across the hexagons to prevent a galactic-scale artifact's activation.

title: Downtiming the Nightside
author: Jack L. Chalker
description: A time-travel agency sends agents upstream and downstream to police paradoxes — until their target begins manipulating history on a larger scale than they can track.

title: Vagabond, Vol. 1
author: Takehiko Inoue
description: Inoue's manga adaptation of Musashi by Eiji Yoshikawa: the young Shinmen Takezo becomes the wandering samurai who will one day be known as Miyamoto Musashi.

title: Slam Dunk, Vol. 1
author: Takehiko Inoue
description: The first volume of Inoue's classic basketball manga: a delinquent high schooler joins the team to impress a girl and slowly discovers an actual love of the game.

title: Real, Vol. 1
author: Takehiko Inoue
description: Inoue's wheelchair-basketball manga: three young men connected by disability and determination build their lives around the sport.

title: Vampire Academy
author: Richelle Mead
description: The first Vampire Academy novel: half-vampire guardian Rose Hathaway protects her best friend, a royal Moroi princess, at a secret boarding school in rural Montana.

title: Frostbite
author: Richelle Mead
description: Vampire Academy #2: a massive Strigoi attack on a royal family prompts the Moroi to reconsider whether the Academy's isolationist defense strategy is enough.

title: Shadow Kiss
author: Richelle Mead
description: Vampire Academy #3: Rose's near-death experience leaves her haunted by the dead while a Strigoi siege threatens the Academy itself.

title: Succubus Blues
author: Richelle Mead
description: The first Georgina Kincaid novel: a Seattle bookstore manager who is secretly a succubus tries to navigate a forbidden relationship with a famous urban fantasy novelist.

title: Some Girls Bite
author: Chloe Neill
description: The first Chicagoland Vampires novel: a University of Chicago grad student attacked in the quad is turned against her will into a vampire of Cadogan House.

title: Friday Night Bites
author: Chloe Neill
description: Chicagoland Vampires #2: Merit is assigned to investigate shapeshifter movements in Chicago and gets drawn into her old social circle's dangerous secrets.

title: Twice Bitten
author: Chloe Neill
description: Chicagoland Vampires #3: a summit of the North American shapeshifters is threatened by attacks that could plunge the city into supernatural war.

title: Moonheart
author: Charles de Lint
description: A young Ottawa woman discovers her ancestral home is a gateway between our world and the faerie world of Tamson House — the founding novel of modern urban fantasy.

title: The Little Country
author: Charles de Lint
description: A Cornish folk musician discovers an unpublished manuscript by an old children's author and finds that the story inside it may be altering her life.

title: Memory and Dream
author: Charles de Lint
description: A painter discovers that the numenlight figures she paints are real — and that the cruel mentor who taught her has been using her to bring dark things across.

title: Swan Song
author: Robert R. McCammon
description: McCammon's post-apocalyptic epic: after a nuclear exchange, a girl with healing powers, a hardened boxer, a vagrant, and a madman move across a ruined America.

title: Boy's Life
author: Robert R. McCammon
description: McCammon's semi-autobiographical coming-of-age novel set in a 1964 Alabama small town: a boy witnesses a murder that opens him to the magic around his childhood.

title: They Thirst
author: Robert R. McCammon
description: McCammon's early vampire epic: a Los Angeles beset by a growing vampire invasion, told through a dozen characters as the city collapses around them.

title: Mine
author: Robert R. McCammon
description: A radicalized Weather Underground fugitive kidnaps a newborn baby believing it to be the child she lost years earlier — the mother must cross America to get him back.

title: The Ceremonies
author: T.E.D. Klein
description: Klein's only novel: a literature professor on sabbatical in rural New Jersey unknowingly becomes the catalyst for an ancient rite that will unmake the world.

title: Dark Gods
author: T.E.D. Klein
description: Klein's collection of four long horror novellas — 'Children of the Kingdom,' 'Petey,' 'Black Man With a Horn,' and 'Nadelman's God.'

title: The Elementals
author: Michael McDowell
description: Three Victorian cottages on an Alabama Gulf beach stand against a dune that is slowly, deliberately, burying one of them — and something is living in the sand.

title: Blackwater
author: Michael McDowell
description: McDowell's six-volume saga of the Caskey family in early 20th-century Alabama, originally published as six paperbacks over five months — Southern Gothic at its most bizarre.

title: Cold Moon Over Babylon
author: Michael McDowell
description: A Florida teenager is murdered in a blueberry grove, and her ghost begins a slow, patient revenge against the family that killed her.

title: The Amulet
author: Michael McDowell
description: McDowell's debut: a cursed amulet passes from hand to hand through a small Alabama town, and anyone who touches it becomes a murderer.

title: The Ritual
author: Adam Nevill
description: Four old friends on a hiking holiday in the Scandinavian wilderness take a shortcut through an ancient forest and find something waiting that should not exist.

title: Last Days
author: Adam Nevill
description: A documentary filmmaker is hired to investigate the Temple of the Last Days, a 1970s cult whose members all died in a remote Arizona ranch — and whose secrets refuse to die.

title: No One Gets Out Alive
author: Adam Nevill
description: A young woman renting a cheap room in a Birmingham boarding house begins hearing voices in the walls — and realizes the landlord knows exactly what they are.

title: Apartment 16
author: Adam Nevill
description: A sealed London apartment has been empty for 50 years, and when a night porter and an American art student investigate, they find it is the center of something the building cannot contain.

title: Something from the Nightside
author: Simon R. Green
description: The first Nightside novel: a reluctant private eye returns to the hidden London district where time runs wrong and gods walk the streets.

title: Hawk & Fisher
author: Simon R. Green
description: The first Hawk & Fisher novel: two tough married guards in the corrupt city of Haven investigate a locked-room murder at a wizard's election-night party.

title: Blue Moon Rising
author: Simon R. Green
description: The first Forest Kingdom novel: the younger prince of a backwater kingdom is sent to fetch a dragon as his rite of passage and winds up with the kingdom's last magical allies.

title: Jhereg
author: Steven Brust
description: The first Vlad Taltos novel: a human assassin in an empire of sorcerous elves gets a contract that is worth a fortune and will certainly get him killed.

title: Yendi
author: Steven Brust
description: Vlad Taltos #2 (chronologically first): the young assassin consolidates his Adrilankha criminal territory and meets his future wife during a violent gang war.

title: Teckla
author: Steven Brust
description: Vlad Taltos #3: Vlad's wife becomes involved with a revolutionary Teckla peasant movement, and Vlad must choose between his wife and his criminal employers.

title: Taltos
author: Steven Brust
description: Vlad Taltos #4 (prequel): the origin story of the assassin Vlad, his familiar Loiosh, and his journey into the Paths of the Dead.

title: Ash: A Secret History
author: Mary Gentle
description: Gentle's alternate-history epic: in 1476 a female mercenary captain fights on the losing side of a forgotten Carthaginian invasion of Europe, narrated in a modern academic frame.

title: Grunts!
author: Mary Gentle
description: A dark-comic military fantasy told from the perspective of an orc raiding party that has inherited the armory of a halfling-style Dead Marine Corps.

title: Rats and Gargoyles
author: Mary Gentle
description: In a Renaissance-like city ruled by giant rat-people and watched by living gargoyles, a spy and a scholar try to prevent an apocalyptic alchemical working.

title: Dragonsbane
author: Barbara Hambly
description: A middle-aged lord and his older witch-lover are summoned from their quiet lives to fight a dragon — and discover the dragon is not the real problem.

title: Those Who Hunt the Night
author: Barbara Hambly
description: A retired Oxford spy is blackmailed by the master vampire of London into investigating who is murdering the city's undead during daylight.

title: The Ladies of Mandrigyn
author: Barbara Hambly
description: The widows of a conquered city hire a mercenary captain to train them as an army to rescue their men — Hambly's early feminist sword-and-sorcery novel.

title: The Magic of Recluce
author: L.E. Modesitt Jr.
description: The first Recluce novel: a young man on an island where order magic is law is exiled for doubting, and must figure out what kind of magician he really is.

title: The Magic Engineer
author: L.E. Modesitt Jr.
description: A Recluce smith exiled for his unusual techniques builds a military engineering empire on the continent — and clashes with the order magic of his homeland.

title: The White Order
author: L.E. Modesitt Jr.
description: A young scribe's son is discovered to have the talents of a chaos mage and is taken from his family to study in the all-white Empire of Fairhaven.

title: Imager
author: L.E. Modesitt Jr.
description: The first Imager Portfolio novel: a young painter in a magical Italianate city-state discovers he is an 'imager' — capable of shaping reality by visualizing it.

title: A Princess of Mars
author: Edgar Rice Burroughs
description: The first Barsoom novel: a Virginia gentleman mysteriously transported to Mars wins the love of an imperial princess and fights across the red planet's dying civilizations.

title: Tarzan of the Apes
author: Edgar Rice Burroughs
description: The origin of Tarzan: an English lord raised by apes in the African jungle after the mysterious death of his parents — Burroughs's most enduring character.

title: The Gods of Mars
author: Edgar Rice Burroughs
description: Barsoom #2: John Carter returns to Mars and discovers the terrible secret of its religion — the river Iss does not lead to paradise but to cannibalistic predators.

title: The Warlord of Mars
author: Edgar Rice Burroughs
description: Barsoom #3: John Carter pursues his captive wife Dejah Thoris across the black pole of Mars to confront the Therns, the False Gods of Barsoom.

title: At the Earth's Core
author: Edgar Rice Burroughs
description: The first Pellucidar novel: an experimental mechanical mole carries two adventurers five hundred miles into the Earth's crust and out into a prehistoric inner world.

title: The Moon Pool
author: A. Merritt
description: An expedition to the Carolines pursues a mysterious force that has been stealing men through a lunar ray in a ruined Micronesian temple — Merritt's landmark weird romance.

title: The Ship of Ishtar
author: A. Merritt
description: A New York antiquarian opens a sealed Babylonian block and is transported onto an ancient ship carrying a priestess of Ishtar and a priest of Nergal locked in eternal combat.

title: Dwellers in the Mirage
author: A. Merritt
description: Merritt's Alaska adventure: a man with a mysterious past is drawn into a hidden Mongolian civilization worshipping a tentacled horror called Khalk'ru.

title: King Solomon's Mines
author: H. Rider Haggard
description: The first Allan Quatermain novel and one of the founding texts of the 'lost world' adventure genre: a great-white-hunter leads an expedition into unexplored Africa.

title: She
author: H. Rider Haggard
description: An English scholar and his foster son journey to a hidden African kingdom ruled by Ayesha, the immortal white queen known as 'She-Who-Must-Be-Obeyed.'

title: Allan Quatermain
author: H. Rider Haggard
description: The direct sequel to King Solomon's Mines: an aging Quatermain launches one final African expedition and discovers a lost white civilization north of Kenya.

title: Battle Royale
author: Koushun Takami
description: Takami's Japanese dystopian novel: a class of Japanese ninth-graders is sent to an abandoned island and forced to kill each other as part of an authoritarian government program.

title: Salt
author: Adam Roberts
description: Roberts's debut: two colony ships crossing interstellar space to a salt-desert planet diverge politically — and the two landing parties begin a conflict that echoes into the new world.

title: On
author: Adam Roberts
description: A young climber lives on a vertical world — everyone clings to a vast wall — and a fall can send him past every culture his people have ever known.

title: Jack Glass
author: Adam Roberts
description: Three locked-room mysteries involving the notorious killer Jack Glass — Roberts's Campbell Award winning homage to Golden Age detective fiction in a future solar system.

title: Chocolat
author: Joanne Harris
description: A mysterious chocolatier and her young daughter arrive at a strict French village just before Lent — and begin a quiet war with the parish priest.

title: The Gospel of Loki
author: Joanne Harris
description: Harris's retelling of Norse mythology from the viewpoint of the trickster Loki — charting his rise in Asgard and his vengeful role in Ragnarok.

title: Runemarks
author: Joanne Harris
description: Five hundred years after the fall of the Norse gods, a girl born with a magical rune on her palm accidentally lets the old gods back into the world.

title: Different Class
author: Joanne Harris
description: A veteran teacher at a private boys' school finds his past returning to haunt him when a former student is appointed headmaster.

title: Tea with the Black Dragon
author: R.A. MacAvoy
description: MacAvoy's debut: a reclusive 50-something Irish-American musician meets a Chinese gentleman staying at her hotel who may be a two-thousand-year-old dragon.

title: Damiano
author: R.A. MacAvoy
description: A young alchemist in 14th-century Italy and his talking dog travel from a war-torn Piedmont to seek the archangel Raphael for his family's sake.

title: The Book of Kells
author: R.A. MacAvoy
description: A modern artist rubbing a medieval Irish stone cross is transported into 10th-century Ireland and must survive in the age of Viking raids.

title: Daggerspell
author: Katharine Kerr
description: The first Deverry novel: three souls — a dweomer-master, a soldier, and a noblewoman — are reincarnated across centuries in a Celtic-inflected fantasy kingdom.

title: Darkspell
author: Katharine Kerr
description: Deverry #2: the reincarnated Jill, now a mercenary woman, finds her magical talent stirring as dark forces begin to gather around her and her lover Rhodry.

title: The Bristling Wood
author: Katharine Kerr
description: Deverry #3: Rhodry, exiled and believed dead, falls into the hands of the savage Westfolk and Jill must journey into the elven lands to find him.

title: The Dragon Revenant
author: Katharine Kerr
description: Deverry #4: the conclusion of the first Deverry act — Rhodry's claim to his kingdom is threatened by Bardekian slavers while Jill finally accepts her destiny as a dweomer master.

title: Daughter of the Forest
author: Juliet Marillier
description: Marillier's debut retelling of the Six Swans fairytale in early medieval Ireland: a silent sister weaves shirts of starwort to break the spell that transformed her brothers into swans.

title: Son of the Shadows
author: Juliet Marillier
description: Sevenwaters #2: a new generation of the Sevenwaters family confronts old enemies and a prophecy that demands a sacrifice none of them is willing to make.

title: Child of the Prophecy
author: Juliet Marillier
description: Sevenwaters #3: a young sorceress trained by her dark father is sent to destroy the Sevenwaters family — the conclusion of the original trilogy.

title: West of Honor
author: Jerry Pournelle
description: An early Falkenberg's Legion novel: a young Marine officer and his mentor fight to hold a colony world for the dying CoDominium on a failing interstellar frontier.

title: Janissaries
author: Jerry Pournelle
description: A CIA-trained mercenary team is abducted by aliens and set down on a medieval planet where they must organize the local kingdoms around their modern firepower.

title: King David's Spaceship
author: Jerry Pournelle
description: A CoDominium colony world must construct a functioning primitive spacecraft before the Imperials arrive — or be downgraded to a lower technological tier forever.

title: The Amulet of Samarkand
author: Jonathan Stroud
description: The first Bartimaeus Trilogy novel: an apprentice magician summons a 5,000-year-old wise-ass djinn and sends him to steal a powerful amulet from a rival mage.

title: The Golem's Eye
author: Jonathan Stroud
description: Bartimaeus #2: Nathaniel, now a rising young magician, is sent to Prague to investigate the theft of an ancient Kabbalistic artifact that animates a golem.

title: Ptolemy's Gate
author: Jonathan Stroud
description: Bartimaeus #3: the magical ruling class of alternate-England is collapsing under a commoner revolt while Nathaniel summons Bartimaeus one last time for a desperate plan.

title: The Screaming Staircase
author: Jonathan Stroud
description: The first Lockwood & Co. novel: in an alternate England overrun by vengeful ghosts, a three-person psychic investigation agency takes on its deadliest job.

title: Artemis Fowl
author: Eoin Colfer
description: The first Artemis Fowl novel: a twelve-year-old criminal mastermind kidnaps a fairy captain of the LEPrecon police force to ransom her for fairy gold.

title: Artemis Fowl: The Arctic Incident
author: Eoin Colfer
description: Artemis Fowl #2: Artemis's father is kidnapped in Russia and Artemis forms an uneasy alliance with the fairies to rescue him.

title: Artemis Fowl: The Eternity Code
author: Eoin Colfer
description: Artemis Fowl #3: Artemis builds a computer from stolen fairy technology and sells it to a Chicago businessman — then must mount a heist to get it back.

title: Artemis Fowl: The Opal Deception
author: Eoin Colfer
description: Artemis Fowl #4: the pixie Opal Koboi escapes her asylum and orchestrates a plan to expose the fairy world to humanity while framing Artemis for the crime.

title: Blood Price
author: Tanya Huff
description: The first Vicki Nelson novel: a Toronto private detective investigates a series of bloody murders and teams up with a vampire who is secretly Henry VIII's illegitimate son.

title: Valor's Choice
author: Tanya Huff
description: The first Confederation novel: Staff Sergeant Torin Kerr's Marines are assigned a ceremonial honor-guard detail on an alien world — and are promptly ambushed.

title: Summon the Keeper
author: Tanya Huff
description: The first Keeper Chronicle: a modern 'Keeper' inherits a Kingston, Ontario bed-and-breakfast that turns out to be sitting on a hole to hell.

title: Already Dead
author: Charlie Huston
description: The first Joe Pitt novel: a hard-boiled Manhattan vampire PI works cases for the city's feuding vampire Clans and gets caught between all of them.

title: No Dominion
author: Charlie Huston
description: Joe Pitt #2: a new drug hitting the vampyre streets of New York is making them feel alive — and Joe is hired to find its source.

title: Half the Blood of Brooklyn
author: Charlie Huston
description: Joe Pitt #3: Joe is sent into the Brooklyn Clans' cross-borough territorial war to negotiate with a Hasidic vampire rabbi who controls half the borough.

title: Hotel Transylvania
author: Chelsea Quinn Yarbro
description: The first Saint-Germain novel: the four-thousand-year-old vampire Count Saint-Germain takes refuge in pre-Revolution Paris and falls in love with a mortal woman.

title: The Palace
author: Chelsea Quinn Yarbro
description: Saint-Germain #2: the vampire Count lives in Renaissance Florence under Medici patronage until Savonarola's fanatics target him as a sorcerer.

title: Blood Games
author: Chelsea Quinn Yarbro
description: Saint-Germain #3: the vampire Count in Nero's Rome, navigating the decadent imperial court and its sadistic games as the Empire begins its slow collapse.

title: A Shadow on the Glass
author: Ian Irvine
description: The first Three Worlds novel: a crippled chronicler and a runaway princess begin a quest across a dying world whose three moons have stopped turning properly.

title: The Tower on the Rift
author: Ian Irvine
description: Three Worlds #2: Karan and Llian travel to the tower above the rift where the Forbidding between worlds has begun to tear open.

title: Dark is the Moon
author: Ian Irvine
description: Three Worlds #3: the war between the Aachim, Faellem, and human civilizations breaks out as the broken world's moon begins to fall.

title: Rhapsody: Child of Blood
author: Elizabeth Haydon
description: The first Symphony of Ages novel: three travelers — a namer, an assassin, and a brutal warrior — flee their island home beneath the world via an ancient tree root.

title: Prophecy: Child of Earth
author: Elizabeth Haydon
description: Symphony of Ages #2: Rhapsody, Achmed, and Grunthor begin to confront the demonic F'dor that has been spreading its corruption through the new world they've arrived in.

title: Destiny: Child of the Sky
author: Elizabeth Haydon
description: Symphony of Ages #3: the first trilogy's conclusion — Rhapsody must choose between her destiny and her love as the F'dor's plans come to fruition.

title: Crown Duel
author: Sherwood Smith
description: A teenage countess from a remote mountain province leads her people in revolt against the corrupt king — the first half of Smith's classic YA fantasy.

title: Court Duel
author: Sherwood Smith
description: The second half of Crown Duel: Meliara must survive the intricate social politics of the new royal court after the war she helped win.

title: Inda
author: Sherwood Smith
description: The first Inda novel: a noble boy at a brutal military academy reveals a tactical genius that makes him a prophesied leader — and a target.

title: Sleeping Giants
author: Sylvain Neuvel
description: The first Themis Files novel: a child falls into a buried giant metal hand in South Dakota, and decades later a team begins assembling the alien robot it belongs to.

title: Waking Gods
author: Sylvain Neuvel
description: Themis Files #2: other giant robots begin arriving on Earth, and the original Themis team must determine whether they are here to help or exterminate humanity.

title: Only Human
author: Sylvain Neuvel
description: Themis Files #3: the conclusion of Neuvel's interrogation-transcript trilogy — a diplomatic journey to the alien homeworld forces a reckoning with what humanity has done.

title: The Test
author: Sylvain Neuvel
description: Neuvel's novella: a British citizenship test takes a sudden, horrifying turn as the examiner begins asking what-would-you-do questions the applicant was never meant to hear.

author: Peter Clines
description: A laid-off copywriter moves into a cheap LA apartment building and discovers that its strangeness — green cockroaches, inexplicable locked rooms — points to something cosmic.

title: The Fold
author: Peter Clines
description: A high school English teacher with perfect recall is hired by DARPA to evaluate a teleportation project at a remote facility — and finds the team hiding something.

title: Dead Moon
author: Peter Clines
description: A near-future lunar graveyard's operators fight an unexpected outbreak — the dead have started coming back on the moon's surface.

title: Ex-Heroes
author: Peter Clines
description: The first Ex-Heroes novel: a handful of superheroes in a zombie-apocalypse Los Angeles protect a fortress of survivors — and try to keep their powers a secret from the undead.

title: Lovecraft Country
author: Matt Ruff
description: A Jim Crow-era Black family in 1950s Chicago travels across the country in search of a missing relative and confronts both the supernatural horrors of a secret cabal and the horrors of American racism.

title: Bad Monkeys
author: Matt Ruff
description: A woman in a psychiatric ward claims to be an agent of a secret organization called Bad Monkeys that hunts and kills evil people — and her interrogator must decide what to believe.

title: Fool on the Hill
author: Matt Ruff
description: Ruff's debut, written during his senior year at Cornell: a dense magical-realist novel set on and under the Cornell campus, populated by sprites, talking dogs, and a writer-in-residence.

title: Mirage
author: Matt Ruff
description: In an alternate 2009 where the United Arab States is the global superpower, a Baghdad homeland security agent investigates Christian terrorists who claim their world is an illusion.

title: Tender Is the Flesh
author: Agustina Bazterrica
description: In a near-future where animal meat has become toxic and humans are bred for legal slaughter, a middle manager at a processing plant receives a living woman as a gift.

title: The Unworthy
author: Agustina Bazterrica
description: In a post-apocalyptic convent, a woman writes her memories on stolen scraps — the only trace of her life before the sisterhood that claimed her.

title: Nineteen Claws and a Black Bird
author: Agustina Bazterrica
description: Bazterrica's story collection: twenty short tales of psychological unease, body horror, and quiet apocalypse from the author of Tender Is the Flesh.

title: The Runelords
author: David Farland
description: The first Runelords novel: in a world where nobles are endowed with the attributes of their willing subjects, a young prince receives his first endowments as his kingdom is invaded.

title: Brotherhood of the Wolf
author: David Farland
description: The Runelords #2: Prince Gaborn, the Earth King, leads the defense of the Seven Kingdoms against Raj Ahten's hordes and the returning Reavers.

title: Wizardborn
author: David Farland
description: The Runelords #3: Gaborn's powers as Earth King wane, and a new wizardborn child threatens to shift the balance of every family of magic in the world.

title: The Lair of Bones
author: David Farland
description: The Runelords #4: the original quartet's conclusion — Gaborn descends into the underworld to confront the oldest Reavers in a final test of the earth magic.

title: The Rats
author: James Herbert
description: Herbert's debut: giant mutant rats emerge from the sewers of bombed-out East London and begin killing people — a cult-classic British horror novel.

title: The Fog
author: James Herbert
description: A yellow fog drifts across England, and everyone who breathes it turns savagely mad — Herbert's second novel and one of the landmarks of 1970s British horror.

title: The Survivor
author: James Herbert
description: The only survivor of a 747 crash in an English village tries to figure out why the dead passengers are appearing to him and what they want.

title: The Magic Cottage
author: James Herbert
description: A young couple move into a remote English cottage with seemingly benevolent magical properties — and discover what the magic is actually for.

title: Psycho
author: Robert Bloch
description: Bloch's short novel about the Bates Motel and its haunted proprietor Norman — the book that inspired Alfred Hitchcock's film and redefined American psychological horror.

title: The Scarf
author: Robert Bloch
description: Bloch's first novel: an itinerant writer strangles women with the same scarf as he moves from city to city — a very early psychological serial-killer novel.

title: American Gothic
author: Robert Bloch
description: Bloch's fictionalized account of H.H. Holmes, the 1893 Chicago World's Fair serial killer, and the young woman journalist who uncovers his 'Murder Castle.'

title: Dies the Fire
author: S.M. Stirling
description: The first Emberverse novel: on a single evening in 1998, a mysterious Change disables every advanced technology on Earth, and the survivors must relearn an iron-age existence.

title: The Protector's War
author: S.M. Stirling
description: Emberverse #2: the Bearkiller and Clan Mackenzie communities of Oregon face the expanding Portland Protective Association as the post-Change world settles into feudalism.

title: A Meeting at Corvallis
author: S.M. Stirling
description: Emberverse #3: the Willamette Valley allies gather at Corvallis for a final confrontation with the Portland Protective Association — the conclusion of the first trilogy.

title: Island in the Sea of Time
author: S.M. Stirling
description: The first Nantucket novel: the whole island is transported back to 1250 BC and must survive — and reshape — Bronze Age civilization.

author: Eric Flint
description: A small West Virginia coal-mining town is mysteriously transported back to 1632 Germany in the middle of the Thirty Years' War — and must survive with 20th-century technology and 17th-century politics.

author: Eric Flint
description: Flint's collaboration with David Weber: the New United States begins building alliances with Gustavus Adolphus while France and England react to the impossible Americans.

title: 1634: The Baltic War
author: Eric Flint
description: 1632 #3: the Baltic war explodes into the series' first major military conflict as France, Denmark, and England ally against the new American-Swedish coalition.

title: Monster Hunter International
author: Larry Correia
description: The first MHI novel: an accountant accidentally kills his boss — who was a werewolf — and is recruited by a private monster-killing contractor with government immunity.

title: Monster Hunter Vendetta
author: Larry Correia
description: MHI #2: Owen Pitt is targeted by an ancient entity that wants him dead, while the MHI team takes on a series of increasingly impossible contracts.

title: Hard Magic
author: Larry Correia
description: The first Grimnoir Chronicles novel: in an alternate 1930s where magical 'actives' are hunted and used, a Heavy and a Healer join a secret organization fighting the Imperium.

title: Son of the Black Sword
author: Larry Correia
description: The first Saga of the Forgotten Warrior: in an Indian-inspired caste society, a black sword-wielding protector discovers the religion he serves has been lying to him.

title: The Guns of the South
author: Harry Turtledove
description: Turtledove's most famous alternate history: South African white-supremacist time travelers arm the Confederacy with AK-47s, winning the Civil War — and then everyone has to live with the consequences.

title: How Few Remain
author: Harry Turtledove
description: The prologue novel to Turtledove's Timeline-191 series: a second US-Confederate war in 1881 plays out differently because of the South's earlier victory.

title: Worldwar: In the Balance
author: Harry Turtledove
description: The first Worldwar novel: in the middle of World War II, a lizard-like alien race invades Earth — and every combatant nation has to decide whether to fight the aliens, each other, or both.

title: Ruled Britannia
author: Harry Turtledove
description: In an alternate 1597 where the Spanish Armada succeeded and conquered England, William Shakespeare is secretly commissioned to write a play to ignite a rebellion.

title: Lamb
author: Christopher Moore
description: The Gospel According to Biff, Christ's Childhood Pal: Moore's comic novel about Jesus's missing years, told by his resurrected best friend — irreverent, kind, and surprisingly tender.

title: Practical Demonkeeping
author: Christopher Moore
description: Moore's debut: a 100-year-old man in a California town is bound to a small green demon who eats only humans he designates — and the demon has just gotten loose.

title: A Dirty Job
author: Christopher Moore
description: A beta-male San Francisco second-hand store owner discovers he's just been hired as a Death Merchant — an unofficial agent helping souls cross over.

title: Fluke
author: Christopher Moore
description: Moore's novel about a marine biologist studying whales off Maui who sees a humpback with 'Bite Me' tattooed on its fluke — and is then swallowed by another whale.

title: Dreamsnake
author: Vonda McIntyre
description: McIntyre's Hugo-winning novel: a healer in a post-apocalyptic future uses genetically engineered snakes to treat illness — until her dreamsnake, the most important of the three, is killed.

title: The Moon and the Sun
author: Vonda McIntyre
description: In the court of Louis XIV, a Jesuit natural historian captures a 'sea monster' — and the Sun King's ward finds the captive mermaid is a thinking person.

title: Starfarers
author: Vonda McIntyre
description: A group of scientists hijack a starship scheduled for decommissioning to flee a militarizing Earth — the first of McIntyre's Starfarers quartet.

title: Medalon
author: Jennifer Fallon
description: The first Demon Child trilogy novel: a young priestess raised by atheist religious authorities discovers she is the half-demon prophesied to destroy them.

title: The Lion of Senet
author: Jennifer Fallon
description: The first Second Sons trilogy novel: in a world with two suns and a political-religious cult of 'goddess worship,' three royal sons navigate a sudden solar eclipse that no one expected.

title: The Immortal Prince
author: Jennifer Fallon
description: The first Tide Lords novel: a convicted murderer taken for hanging turns out to be literally immortal — and his immortality is tied to ancient tide-aligned lords about to return.

title: John Dies at the End
author: David Wong
description: Two dropout slackers discover a drug called Soy Sauce that lets them perceive other dimensions — and realize they can see the inter-dimensional monsters that are now watching them back.

title: This Book Is Full of Spiders
author: David Wong
description: John Dies at the End #2: invisible spiders are possessing humans across the country, and the Soy Sauce duo must figure out what the spiders actually want.

title: What the Hell Did I Just Read
author: David Wong
description: John Dies at the End #3: small children are disappearing from town, and three narrators — David, John, and Amy — try to explain what actually happened.

title: The Chalk Man
author: C.J. Tudor
description: Tudor's debut: in 1986 a group of English children find a dismembered body using their secret chalk-stick-figure code — and thirty years later someone is sending them chalk men again.

title: The Hiding Place
author: C.J. Tudor
description: A teacher returns to the grim mining town where his sister died as a child — and the old playground game that caught them both in its grip is starting again.

title: The Other People
author: C.J. Tudor
description: A man sees his dead daughter's face through a car window on a highway — and begins a three-year search for the people who took her.

title: The Burning Girls
author: C.J. Tudor
description: A troubled vicar with a dark past is reassigned to a tiny English village whose history includes the Marian burnings of Protestants — and whose present includes fresh violence.

title: The Haunting of Ashburn House
author: Darcy Coates
description: A young woman inherits a remote country house from her reclusive great-aunt and discovers the house's terrible history is still unfolding.

title: Hunted
author: Darcy Coates
description: A group of friends searching for a missing hiker in a remote Australian forest discover they are not the only ones in the woods.

title: From Below
author: Darcy Coates
description: A deep-sea dive team is hired to explore the wreck of a WWII-era cargo ship at the edge of human-survivable depth — and finds something has been waiting for them.

title: Behind Her Eyes
author: Sarah Pinborough
description: A single mother befriends the beautiful wife of her boss — and finds herself at the center of a psychological thriller with a notoriously unexpected ending.

title: Dead to Her
author: Sarah Pinborough
description: A Savannah socialite's husband brings home a young Black second wife — and Marcie's polite tolerance begins to slip into something darker.

title: Cross Her Heart
author: Sarah Pinborough
description: Three women — a mother, a daughter, and the mother's best friend — hold secrets that begin to surface when the daughter is nearly killed in an accident.

title: Pavane
author: Keith Roberts
description: Roberts's alt-history fix-up: Queen Elizabeth I is assassinated in 1588, the Spanish Armada wins, and by the 20th century England is a technologically stagnant Catholic province.

title: The Chalk Giants
author: Keith Roberts
description: Linked stories set on a post-apocalyptic English coast — the chalk-cliff giants bear witness as civilization recedes and re-emerges as myth.

title: Berserker
author: Fred Saberhagen
description: The first Berserker fix-up: giant sentient machines designed by a long-dead civilization hunt and kill all organic life across the galaxy — and humans must figure out how to stop them.

title: The Broken Lands
author: Fred Saberhagen
description: The first Empire of the East novel: in a far-future post-magical-holocaust Earth, a young resistance member fights an empire that rules through elemental demons.

title: The Holmes-Dracula File
author: Fred Saberhagen
description: Saberhagen's mash-up: Count Dracula arrives in Victorian London just as Sherlock Holmes is investigating a vampire case — and the two must collaborate.

title: First Contact
author: Murray Leinster
description: Leinster's landmark novella (published as a collection): two starships from different civilizations meet in deep space and must solve the problem of how to part without telling each other where home is.

title: The Forgotten Planet
author: Murray Leinster
description: A planet seeded with Earth biology is forgotten for generations — and the descendants of its survivors regress to pre-stone-age life among giant insects.

title: Martians, Go Home
author: Fredric Brown
description: Brown's comic satire: a billion obnoxious green Martians appear on Earth overnight and refuse to leave, broadcasting people's secrets and heckling everyone within earshot.

title: What Mad Universe
author: Fredric Brown
description: An SF magazine editor is accidentally transported into the kind of pulp space opera he's been rejecting — and discovers he can't get back out.

title: The Lights in the Sky Are Stars
author: Fredric Brown
description: A 60-year-old engineer in the near-future struggles to build public support for a manned mission to Jupiter — Brown's quiet late novel about space advocacy.

title: Shadowshaper
author: Daniel José Older
description: A Brooklyn teenager discovers her Puerto Rican family's tradition of 'shadowshaping' — merging spirits into art — and is hunted by someone who wants to steal it.

title: Half-Resurrection Blues
author: Daniel José Older
description: The first Bone Street Rumba novel: a half-dead Brooklyn PI works for the Council of the Dead and investigates a case that points toward something older than the afterlife.

title: Dactyl Hill Squad
author: Daniel José Older
description: In an alternate Civil War Brooklyn with dinosaurs, five orphans of color lead a resistance against slave catchers who are kidnapping children of color into the war effort.

title: A for Anything
author: Damon Knight
description: A matter duplicator called a 'Gismo' is invented — and civilization collapses into neo-feudalism as the gift of infinite abundance makes every form of labor worthless.

title: Beyond the Barrier
author: Damon Knight
description: An amnesiac college professor discovers he was transported from the far future to escape an alien enemy — and the enemy has followed him back.
